- **From:** "Paul Raulerson" <praulerson@NOSPAM-hot.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2002-03-30T15:43:27+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<z2lp8.99995$Vl.3446530@typhoon.austin.rr.com>`
- **References:** `<SBbp8.138705$af7.70302@rwcrnsc53>`

```
Your hardware background will probably be an advantage to you in some languages and environments,
and a distinct dis-advantage in others. So let's try this the real way. 

(1) Pick something interesting and exciting that you want to do on a computer. Everyone is different. I enjoy writing billing software. I also get a kick out of writing real time control software, that sort of defines what kind of projects I tend to like the most. In any event pick something you really think would be cool.

(2) Evaluate what platform it would be best implemented on. (You can expect some help from the group here.) 

(3) Evaluate and decide what language/software is appropriate and the best fit for the project. Again, you can expect help from here, though you should expect it will be weighted somewhat towards a COBOL style solution. If it turns out what
you really want to do is better done in C, we can also point you towards the appropriate C-Language (or whatever language
it winds up to be) group. 

Number one is all yours however - and it is often the most difficult part of any project. Choose wisely, if you don't follow it through and complete it, it will be wasted effort, and the start of a bad habit that is difficult to break. 

-Paul



"Greg" <SinfulGreg@hotmail.com> wrote in message news:SBbp8.138705$af7.70302@rwcrnsc53...
> Network Blitz
> I would like to know if someone could explain what I need Software wise to
> start learning programming?
```

## Thread: Off-topic: Rephrasing "employment terminated"? — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Off-topic and spam`](topics.md#spam)_

### Re: Off-topic: Rephrasing "employment terminated"?

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2002-03-30T11:57:40+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<UKhp8.825$tW2.366@nwrddc04.gnilink.net>`
- **References:** `<a7o5d1$4jga$1@mercury.vcu.edu>`

```
<bgwillia@vcu.edu> wrote in message news:a7o5d1$4jga$1@mercury.vcu.edu...
> Hi,

>  I noticed that I had to use the phrase "employment terminated" when I got
fired from a job thirteen years ago ..
>... a hiring director would now see the phrase "employment  terminated" as
a red flag on the form.
>
> Can anyong suggest a way of rephrasing or softening the phrase
> "employement terminated" without lying?

Do you really want to work for someone who cares about something which
happened thirteen years ago? The fact you loyally remained with your current
employer during a period of incredible turnover for the NEXT thirteen years
seems far more significant.

But if you have your heart committed to sharing the circumstances of your
departure, tell the truth: your job was eliminated. (*the* job was not
eliminated, but *yours* was).( Or is that too 'Clintonesque?').

MCM
```

## Thread: evaluate problem — 5 this month / 12 total

_Topics: [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax)_

### evaluate problem

- **From:** "Bj���rn M���ller" <G7b9@aol.com>
- **Date:** 2002-03-31T20:48:07+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a87los$q1r5j$1@ID-63521.news.dfncis.de>`

```
hi everybody,

i have a problem with an "evaluate"  in my program.
The first time i pass the evaluate everythings fine and it works great.
but at the second time i always get the error-message "119 Name is not
unique".
At this time it does not matter wich progam ive called previous or wich im
calling now.
Can anybody help me with that??

here is the part with the evaluate:
--------------------------
           accept fun at 2051 with
                           foreground-color 14
                           background-color 1.

           evaluate fun
               when "N"
               when "n" call "neuzugang"
               when "A"
               when "a" call "anzeigen"
               when "B"
               when "b" call "change"
               when "L"
               when "l" call "loeschen"
               when "S"
               when "s" go to hv
               when other go to auswahl.
------------------------------

thx
bjoern
```

#### ↳ Re: evaluate problem

- **From:** "Paul Raulerson" <praulerson@NOSPAM-hot.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2002-03-31T21:03:40+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<MQKp8.86179$Dv6.3474678@typhoon.austin.rr.com>`
- **References:** `<a87los$q1r5j$1@ID-63521.news.dfncis.de>`

```
You probably need to put an specific END-EVALUATE in there to terminate the
statement.
When you do that, expect to have something else misbehave - you probably
have some scoping
error in there.

-Paul


"Bj�rn M�ller" <G7b9@aol.com> wrote in message
news:a87los$q1r5j$1@ID-63521.news.dfncis.de...
> hi everybody,
>
> i have a problem with an "evaluate"  in my program.
```

#### ↳ Re: evaluate problem

- **From:** jnjsle@aol.com (Jnjsle)
- **Date:** 2002-03-31T23:37:15+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20020331183715.26426.00000628@mb-ff.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<a87los$q1r5j$1@ID-63521.news.dfncis.de>`

```
Hi Bjorn,

I speak as an IBM MVS Cobol user when I say that end-eval is not required to
end an eval stmt. I'm not sure of other Cobol implementations. 

In addition, MVS Cobol will recognize only the first 8 characters of a pgm name
used in a CALL stmt. I noticed that at least one of your CALLs uses a 9
character module name. If your version of Cobol truncates the pgm name to 8
chars, and your system carries multiple modules that begin with those 8 chars,
this may be the cause of the error and accompanying message.

Regards, Jack.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Program Names - in MVS (was: evaluate problem

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-03-31T18:04:37-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a888g0$v8u$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<a87los$q1r5j$1@ID-63521.news.dfncis.de> <20020331183715.26426.00000628@mb-ff.aol.com>`

```
Just so others aren't confused.

The 8-character program-name limit has been removed in recent IBM Mainframe
compilers - although the DEFAULT is still an 8-character (7 - I think in
VSE) limit.

For support for longer and mixed Upper-/Lower-case support, see the PGMNAME
compiler option described at:

  http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/cgi-bin/bookmgr/BOOKS/igy3pg00/2.4.39?

        and/or

 http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/cgi-bin/bookmgr/BOOKS/igy3lr00/3.1.1?
```

#### ↳ Re: evaluate problem

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-03-31T18:14:22-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a888p4$n6j$1@slb0.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<a87los$q1r5j$1@ID-63521.news.dfncis.de>`

```
Others have talked about adding an END-EVALUATE statement,  I don't think
that is the problem - as it is optional and this EVALUATE ends with a period
(full-stop).

However, I am a little confused by exactly what the reported problem is.
The original note says,

 "but at the second time i always get the error-message "119 Name is not
unique".

a message that says "name is not unique" sounds like a COMPILE-TIME message.
However, talking about "the second time" sounds like a run-time message.

Is this a compile-time message?  If so, can you show us the code showing
"both" the 1st and 2nd "time" you "use" this?

P.S.  What compiler and run-time environment is showing this problem? This
might help us figure out the real problem. I know that this is NOT MVS (or
any IBM mainframe) because you use ACCEPT .. AT, so whoever answered with
that "assumed" must be mistaken.

P.P.S.  If you have a compiler with intrinsic functions, are you aware that
you can change:

            evaluate fun
              when "N"
              when "n" call "neuzugang"

to

     Evaluate Function Upper-Case (fun)
         When "N"
             call "neuzugang"
 ...
```

### FROM LEARNING PROBLEMS TO FAMILY CRISES.

- **From:** evabooh@hotmail.com (Eva)

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<f7b1078d.0203311603.127726e4@posting.google.com>`

```
One of my sons is reluctant to produce any good school work, and has 
turned into a frightful headache for the family's, despite being very 
intelligent and creative, and a good reader too. 
I've tried other alternatives, with psychologists, and psychopedagoges, 
even private teachers, but nothing seems to work. 
Please, has anyone a suggestion to make?
```

#### ↳ Re: FROM LEARNING PROBLEMS TO FAMILY CRISES.

- **From:** stephenjspiro@mail.com (Stephen J Spiro)
- **Date:** 2002-03-31T21:14:49-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<928495c6.0203312114.6623867b@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<f7b1078d.0203311603.127726e4@posting.google.com>`

```
First, have him checked thoroughly by a medical doctor.  Possible causes are

nutrition
allergies
diseases
biochemistry
physical damage to any of all sorts of things
glandular
alcohol or drugs
biochemical-based mental disorder
etc...

Good luck and God bless your family.

Stephen J Spiro
```
